methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

data.narrative:
Magic AI, a Series C AI & Software company, is valued at $1.5B+ post-money following $466M in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), all operating in AI & Software at similar or advanced stages.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, implying $2.5B. Peer-median analysis examines 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Figure AI ($39.0B), Skild AI ($14.0B), Neuralink ($9.7B), Luma AI ($4.0B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), and one additional peer—yielding a median of $6.8B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B.

The blended analysis supports a valuation range of $2.2B to $7.9B, with a median of $4.7B. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the lower bound, reflecting the early-stage capital deployment relative to peer comparables. Key uncertainties center on near-term revenue trajectory and capital efficiency relative to the median peer cohort, which exhibits substantial dispersion driven by differing product commercialization timelines and investor sentiment toward frontier AI applications. The wide peer range ($1.5B–$39.0B) underscores valuation sensitivity to differentiation and execution visibility within this nascent sector.
